svg.title: How data curation and RLHF reduce bias in LLMs
svg.desc: Two-stage diagram. Top half shows data curation upstream of training — filtering toxic content, balancing sources, deduplication. Bottom half shows the RLHF loop: model outputs rated by humans, a reward model trained on ratings, and the policy model updated via PPO to produce less biased responses.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What kinds of bias exist in raw internet training data?')
sendPrompt('How is toxic content filtered from LLM training data?')
sendPrompt('Why does source diversity matter in LLM training data?')
sendPrompt('What is deduplication in LLM training data?')
sendPrompt('What does clean training data look like for an LLM?')
sendPrompt('What is a pre-trained base LLM?')
sendPrompt('How does the LLM generate candidate outputs in RLHF?')
sendPrompt('What do human raters do in RLHF?')
sendPrompt('What is a reward model in RLHF?')
sendPrompt('How does PPO update the LLM policy in RLHF?')
